name: Mon State (Myanmar)
description: Flag of Mon State, a red field bearing a yellow hamsa (sacred goose) — the long-standing emblem of the Mon people.
history: Used by the Mon State government as one of Myanmar's 14 first-level administrative subdivisions (seven regions and seven states, plus Naypyidaw Union Territory). Many regional and state flag designs follow a common pattern based on the national flag, charged with the regional seal.

features.symbolism: the long-standing emblem of the Mon people, derived from the legendary founding of the city of Bago / Hanthawaddy
